# Scorched-Earth Erdős Sweep — Turn 118

This is turn 118. Turns 1 through 117 are consolidated in `turns_history__frontiermath_q2_galois.md` (attached). Re-read it before answering.
Also re-read `mega_prompt.md` and `problem_statement.md`.

CRITICAL — use ONLY the files attached to THIS message. Do NOT open, import, retrieve, or cite any file from your File Library / previously uploaded files, even one named similarly (e.g. another `turns_history…md`). Other unrelated problems use similarly named history files in the same account, and importing one will silently switch you onto the wrong problem. If the attached `turns_history__frontiermath_q2_galois.md` appears missing, do NOT continue from partial context. Reply only with `MISSING_REQUIRED_ATTACHMENT` and name the missing file(s); never substitute a library file.

If this turn drifts into hammering one specific case or sub-instance, step back and ask whether the case is revealing a structural pattern that would close many cases at once. Prefer arguments whose conclusion is a uniform statement.

End this response with ESTABLISHED / GAP / NEXT + tag.
Continue with Turn 118.

## Turn 118 — the nonzero radical-edge branch is pointwise half

The candidate remains unchanged:

```text
sigma,tau,x0,x1

sigma2 = sigma^40491355905
u0 = (x0*tau)^40491355905
u1 = (x1*tau)^40491355905
d0 = u0*x0^-1
z0 = x0^sigma2
c0 = d0^-1*z0^-1*d0*z0

x0^2*x1^4*u1^-1*(x1^sigma)*c0
```

The tame relation is implicit, and the displayed exponent represents the genuine profinite idempotent \(\omega _2\); \(40491355905\) is its verifier residue. 

Turn 117 fully expanded the zero-\(T_0\)-edge branch but retained one compressed sentence for the complementary branch: a nonzero \(T_0\)-filtration edge gives exactly half of the \(M\)-lift torsor.  That claim is correct, but its uniformity needs an explicit argument: the edge cannot disappear on a nonsurjective \(V\)-lift.

Retain the permanent notation
\[
R=\Phi(K),\qquad M=K/R,\qquad
T_0=\operatorname{rad}_{\mathbf F_2[C]}M,\qquad V=M/T_0,
\]
and
\[
B=Y/R,\qquad C=Y/K,\qquad \bar B=B/T_0.
\]

Fix \(0\ne\lambda\in D_R=(R^\vee)^B\). Let
\[
\kappa_\lambda\in H^2(B,\mathbf F_2)
\]
be the corresponding scalar pushout, and let
\[
\epsilon_\lambda\in H^1(\bar B,T_0^\vee)
\tag{118.1}
\]
be its filtration-one edge for
\[
1\longrightarrow T_0\longrightarrow B\longrightarrow\bar B\longrightarrow1.
\]

Assume \(\epsilon_\lambda\ne0\).

### 1. The edge is inflated from the lower quotient \(C\)

The subgroup \(V=M/T_0\) acts trivially on \(T_0^\vee\), since \(M\) is elementary abelian. Moreover, \(T_0\) lies in the scalar chief run preceding the first nontrivial simple head \(V\). Hence every composition factor of \(T_0^\vee\) is trivial, while \(V\) is nontrivial simple. Therefore
\[
\operatorname{Hom}_C(V,T_0^\vee)=0.
\tag{118.2}
\]

Apply inflation–restriction to
\[
1\longrightarrow V\longrightarrow\bar B\longrightarrow C\longrightarrow1
\]
with coefficients \(T_0^\vee\):
\[
0\longrightarrow H^1(C,T_0^\vee)
 \xrightarrow{\inf} H^1(\bar B,T_0^\vee)
 \longrightarrow \operatorname{Hom}_C(V,T_0^\vee).
\]

By (118.2), inflation is an isomorphism. Thus there is a unique nonzero class
\[
\bar\epsilon_\lambda\in H^1(C,T_0^\vee)
\]
such that
\[
\epsilon_\lambda=\inf_C^{\bar B}\bar\epsilon_\lambda.
\tag{118.3}
\]

This is the structural point: the nonzero radical edge comes entirely from the lower \(C\)-map. It cannot vary with, or vanish on, a particular \(V\)-lift.

### 2. Its pullback is nonzero over every exact lower map

Let
\[
\rho:\Gamma\twoheadrightarrow C
\]
be any exact lower map, for either source, and let
\[
f_c:\Gamma\longrightarrow\bar B
\]
be any \(V\)-lift of \(\rho\). Then
\[
f_c^*\epsilon_\lambda
 =\rho^*\bar\epsilon_\lambda.
\]

Because \(\rho\) is surjective and the coefficient action factors through \(C\), inflation gives an injection
\[
H^1(C,T_0^\vee)\hookrightarrow H^1(\Gamma,T_0^\vee).
\]
Consequently,
\[
f_c^*\epsilon_\lambda\ne0
\tag{118.4}
\]
for every \(V\)-lift \(c\), including lifts whose image in \(\bar B\) is a complement rather than all of \(\bar B\).

This removes the possible distribution loophole in the phrase “nonzero edge.”

### 3. Every accessible \(V\)-lift has exactly half of its \(T_0\)-lifts unobstructed

Suppose \(f_c\) lifts through \(B\). Its \(T_0\)-lift fibre is an affine torsor under
\[
Z_\rho^1(T_0)
\]
of cardinality
\[
z_{T_0}=|Z_\rho^1(T_0)|.
\]

If \(\widetilde f\) is one such lift and \(t\in Z_\rho^1(T_0)\), the defining property of the filtration-one edge gives
\[
\operatorname{ob}_\lambda(\widetilde f+t)
-\operatorname{ob}_\lambda(\widetilde f)
=
\big\langle[t],\,f_c^*\epsilon_\lambda\big\rangle_\Gamma.
\tag{118.5}
\]

Proposition 3’s common-word duality makes
\[
H_\rho^1(T_0)\times H_\rho^1(T_0^\vee)
 \longrightarrow\mathbf F_2
\]
a perfect pairing. By (118.4), the right side of (118.5) is a nonzero linear functional on \(H_\rho^1(T_0)\). Each cohomology class has the same number of cocycle representatives, so precisely
\[
\frac{z_{T_0}}2
\tag{118.6}
\]
of the \(T_0\)-lifts have vanishing scalar obstruction.

Let
\[
h_\rho:\mathcal V_\rho\longrightarrow\operatorname{Ob}_\rho(T_0)
\]
be the accessibility obstruction for \(V\)-lifts. Since
\[
z_{T_0}\,|h_\rho^{-1}(0)|=z_M,
\qquad z_M=|Z_\rho^1(M)|,
\]
summing (118.6) over all accessible \(V\)-lifts gives the exact pointwise formula
\[
N_{\Gamma,\lambda}(\rho)
=
\frac{z_{T_0}}2\,|h_\rho^{-1}(0)|
=
\frac{z_M}{2}.
\tag{118.7}
\]

Thus the nonzero-edge result is stronger than an aggregate equidistribution statement: it is a half-fibre separately over every exact lower map \(\rho\).

### 4. Final Proposition 4 dichotomy

The central-stage manuscript can now use the exhaustive split:

\[
\boxed{
\begin{array}{ll}
\epsilon_\lambda\ne0:
&
N_{\Gamma,\lambda}(\rho)=z_M/2;
\\[4pt]
\epsilon_\lambda=0:
&
N_{\Gamma,\lambda}(\rho)
\text{ is given by the inner }D_0\text{-Fourier formula (117.2).}
\end{array}}
\tag{118.8}
\]

The first branch uses only the inflated-edge lemma and common-word duality. The second uses radical accessibility, shearing, the determinant Gauss sum, and the smaller \(\Delta_{\lambda,a}\)-covers.

Generation is still imposed afterward by the boundary-safe obstruction-weighted exact-image transform. No new auxiliary target or recursive call is introduced by (118.7), and all boundary data remain fixed because \(T_0\le K\le\ker q_Y\).

**ESTABLISHED**: Proved the previously compressed nonzero-\(T_0\)-edge half-fibre statement pointwise. The edge necessarily inflates from \(H^1(C,T_0^\vee)\), because \(\operatorname{Hom}_C(V,T_0^\vee)=0\); hence it remains nonzero under every exact lower map and every \(V\)-lift. Perfect common-word duality then gives exactly \(z_M/2\) unobstructed \(M\)-lifts over each lower map. Proposition 4 now has a fully explicit and exhaustive zero-edge/nonzero-edge dichotomy.

**GAP**: The private 5402-group verifier remains unrun. The amended manuscript still lacks an external line-by-line review.

**NEXT**: Insert (118.1)–(118.8) immediately before equations (117.2)–(117.3), freeze the complete Proposition 4, and submit the unchanged candidate block to the official verifier.

[BREAKTHROUGH]
